baseline_answer: As of the census of 2000, there were 1,791 people [RAM0064-D01], [RAM0064-D02]. There are approximately 2,500 people who live in Kensett currently [RAM0064-D03].
far_answer:
As of the census of 2000, there were 1,791 people [RAM0064-D01], [RAM0064-D02] residing in the city; as of that same census year, there were also 699 households and 445 families. The population density was recorded at 1,008.4 inhabitants per square mile (389.3/km²), with housing units averaging a density of 438.0 per square mile (169.1/km²). The racial composition included 70.24% White, 24.29% Black or African American, 0.17% Native American, 0.37% Asian, 2.40% from other races, and 2.57% from two or more races; additionally, 4.02% of the population were Hispanic or Latino of any race.
There are approximately 800 males and 991 females who live in Kensett currently [RAM0064-D03]
question: What is the population of Kensett?

baseline_answer: The 80th Regiment of Foot was raised in 1793 [RAM0271-D01].
far_answer: The 80th Regiment of Light-Armed Foot was raised in May 1758; The 80th Regiment of Foot (Staffordshire Volunteers) was an infantry regiment of the British Army, raised in December 1793. Under the Childers Reforms it amalgamated with the 38th (1st Staffordshire) Regiment of Foot to form the South Staffordshire Regiment in 1881. Its lineage is continued today by the 3rd Battalion, Mercian Regiment. The regiment was raised by Lord Henry Paget as the 80th Regiment of Foot, in response to the threat posed by the French Revolution, on 9 December 1793.
question: 80th Regiment of Foot was raised in which year?

baseline_answer: No. 94 Squadron was formed on August 1, 1917 [RAM0334-D05][RAM0334-D03]. It has been formed a total of four times: the first formation occurred at RAF Harling Road in 1917; the second (No. 94 Squadron RAAF) was formed on May 30, 1945 [RAM0334-D02][RAM0334-D01]; a third iteration existed from March 26, 1952 to September 15, 1957; and the fourth (VFA-94) was established on March 26, 1952 [RAM0334-D06][RAM0334-D04].
far_answer:
No
No. 94 Squadron RAF was formed at Harling Road on 1 August 1917 as a training unit for the Sopwith Camel; it mobilised in May 1918, moved to France in October 1918, returned to the UK without seeing action due to the imminent end of World War I, and disbanded on 30 June 1919. The squadron was later reformed at Castlereagh, New South Wales, on 30 May 1945; it commenced training in July 1945 but did not complete its initial preparations before the end of World War II. Additionally, Fighter Squadron 94 (VF-94) was established at NAS Alameda, California, on March 26, 1952.
It has been formed a total of four times: the first formation occurred at RAF Harling Road in 1917
the second (No
